dimensionality: 0D phase point on Poincaré disk boundary S¹
action_formula: S_ν = (1 + L₄/L₃) · ħ/2 = 9/14 (bare), with calibration: S₁ = 9/14 + OI·2/49 − OI²·2/49

mass_formula.action_difference: S_k = S₁ − OI·ln(r_k) where r = [1, L₄, L₃+L₄+1] = [1, 2, 10]

hypothesis.geometry: Tetrahedron with vertices Î, M̂, Â_μ, Â_τ
hypothesis.edges: 6 edges = 3 angles + 1 CP phase + 2 mass splittings
hypothesis.calibration_origin: dS/OI = (L₄/L₃)²/2 × (1−OI) — projected face area of tetrahedron
hypothesis.preference_dynamics: Heisenberg mode (ħ/2) for base action, zeta mode (π²) for m₃ scale
hypothesis.dimension: 0D — no Legendre 3-square, spectral zeta, or Kepler mapping
